questions.question: A spacecraft travels $240$ miles in $4$ hours. What is its unit rate?
A laboratory experiment requires a $3:5$ ratio of chemical A to chemical B. If $9$ units of chemical A are used, how many units of chemical B are needed?
The force of gravity on an object is directly proportional to its mass. If a $2$-kilogram object has a gravitational force of $20$ Newtons, what is the gravitational force on a $5$-kilogram object?
A rocket travels $1200$ kilometers in $10$ hours. What is its average speed?
The cost of launching a satellite is directly proportional to its weight. If a $100$-kilogram satellite costs $$1000$ to launch, what is the cost of launching a $250$-kilogram satellite?

A planet's orbital period is inversely proportional to the square root of its distance from the sun. If a planet with an orbital period of $10$ months is $4$ astronomical units from the sun, what is the orbital period of a planet $9$ astronomical units from the sun?
A team of astronauts can assemble a space station in $18$ days. If $6$ astronauts work on the project, how many days will it take for $12$ astronauts to assemble the same station?
A space probe travels $6000$ kilometers in $5$ hours. What is its unit rate?

A physics experiment involves a direct variation between the force applied to an object and its resulting acceleration. If $5$ Newtons of force result in an acceleration of $2$ meters per second squared, find the force required to produce an acceleration of $8$ meters per second squared. Show all work and explain your answer.

A spacecraft's fuel consumption is inversely proportional to its speed. If $200$ kilograms of fuel are consumed when traveling at $5000$ kilometers per hour, how much fuel will be consumed when traveling at $10,!000$ kilometers per hour? Use the given information to write an equation and solve for the unknown fuel consumption. Provide your final answer in kilograms.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of units in rate problems.
Encourage students to draw diagrams to visualize direct and inverse variation.
Remind students to check their units when converting between different measurement systems.
